alignments.preprint_claim_text: Memristor crossbars demonstrate a small footprint and remarkably high energy efficiency in computer vision models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Memristor crossbars demonstrate a small footprint and remarkably high energy efficiency in computer vision models.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim about memristor crossbars' footprint and energy efficiency in computer vision models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel architecture for the memristor crossbar enables the deployment of state-of-the-art large language models on a single chip or package.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed novel architecture for the memristor crossbar enables the deployment of state-of-the-art large language models on a single chip or package.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution regarding enabling LLM deployment on a single chip/package.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our testing on BERT_Large showed negligible accuracy loss.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Testing on BERT showed negligible accuracy loss with the new architecture.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result (negligible accuracy loss on BERT); the model naming is slightly different but the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our architecture achieves enhancements of up to 39X in area overhead and 18X in energy consumption compared to traditional memristor crossbars.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new architecture achieves enhancements in area overhead and energy consumption compared to traditional memristor crossbars.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the direction of the claim but omits the specific numerical magnitudes, reducing claim strength and specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Compared to modern TPU/GPU systems, our architecture demonstrates at least a 68X reduction in the area-delay product and a significant 69% energy consumption reduction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Compared to modern TPU/GPU systems, the architecture demonstrates at least a reduction in the area-delay product and a significant 69% reduction in energy consumption.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the energy reduction figure but removes the explicit "68X" area-delay product figure, weakening and reducing specificity of the original quantitative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hawk is an efficient and accurate NALM system that operates in two stages: dataset construction and event recognition.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the system-level description of Hawk as a two-stage NALM system; no matching statement appears in the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HawkDATA takes only 1/71.5 of the collection time to collect 6.34x more appliance state combinations than the baseline.
alignments.pub_claim_text: HawkDATA takes only 1/71.5 of the collection time to collect 6.34x more appliance state combinations than the baseline.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this concrete measurement claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hawk achieves an average F1 score of 93.94% for state recognition and 97.07% for event recognition, which is a 47.98% and 11.57% increase over SOTA algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hawk achieves an average F1 score of 93.94% for state recognition and 97.07% for event recognition, which is a 47.98% and 11.57% increase over SOTA algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same performance numbers and comparative improvements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In two real-world scenarios with many unknown background appliances, the average F1 scores of event recognition are 96.02% and 94.76%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average F1 scores of event recognition in two real-world scenarios are 96.02% and 94.76%.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same real-world evaluation F1 scores; the context is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLaMEA-HPO achieves superior or comparable performance compared to existing LLM-driven frameworks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLaMEA-HPO achieves superior or comparable performance compared to existing LLM-driven frameworks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this comparative performance claim identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The LLaMEA-HPO framework reduces the number of required LLM queries and improves the overall efficiency of the optimization process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LLaMEA-HPO framework reduces the number of required LLM queries and improves the overall efficiency of the optimization process.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This work highlights the importance of separating algorithmic innovation and structural code search from parameter tuning in LLM-driven code optimization.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same conclusion about separating concerns in LLM-driven code optimization.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Introducing a sleep phase significantly enhanced accuracy in models trained with limited data.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same experimental result regarding a sleep phase improving accuracy with limited data.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Sleep replay rescued previously learned information that had been forgotten following new task training.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sleep replay often enhanced performance in prior tasks, especially those trained with limited data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Sleep replay often enhanced performance in prior tasks, especially those trained with limited data.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This study highlights the multifaceted role of sleep replay in augmenting learning efficiency and facilitating continual learning in ANNs.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Using chapter titles can enhance the effectiveness of sparse retrieval in search tasks.

alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate the specific architectural mechanisms (dilated convolutions and contrastive-based transformers), so the mechanistic claim is omitted from the listed publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adversarial-based data augmentation mitigates the skewed distribution of chest X-rays in the dataset.

alignments.pub_claim_text: XCCNet enhances the early diagnosis of pneumonia, potentially saving many children’s lives.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional image processing methods struggle to capture distinct features in chest radiographs due to low-intensity radiation.
alignments.rationale: This limitation/observation about traditional methods is presented in the publication but was not in the provided preprint claim list.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed multimodal information bottleneck model effectively learns task-relevant joint representations from egocentric images and proprioception.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution verbatim, with no substantive change in meaning or confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method achieves better sample efficiency and zero-shot robustness to unseen white noise than leading baselines in challenging locomotion tasks.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Leveraging information from both egocentric images and proprioception is more beneficial for learning policies on locomotion tasks than using a single modality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed CNFs-based unsupervised LDCT iterative reconstruction algorithm effectively avoids detail loss and secondary artifacts by employing strict one-way transformation during alternating optimization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel unsupervised conditionalization strategy allows for training CNFs on high-resolution CT images, leading to fast and high-quality unsupervised reconstruction.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not explicitly assert that the proposed conditionalization strategy enables training on high-resolution CTs or emphasizes speed/quality in that phrasing, so this specific claim is absent from the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on different datasets suggest that the performance of the proposed algorithm could surpass some state-of-the-art unsupervised and even supervised methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results across multiple datasets demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms several state-of-the-art unsupervised methods and even rivals some supervised approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same comparative experimental outcome but states it more definitively (demonstrates/outperforms) compared with the preprint's more tentative phrasing, increasing confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep-learning techniques have demonstrated significant potential in low-dose computed tomography (LDCT) reconstruction.
alignments.rationale: This general contextual claim about deep learning's potential in LDCT appears in the publication but was not listed among the preprint claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Current methods for conditionalization in CNFs rely on labeled data, leaving unsupervised CNF-based LDCT reconstruction an unresolved challenge.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach to data-free knowledge distillation through diverse diffusion augmentation significantly reduces dependency on original training data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed diverse diffusion augmentation (DDA) approach improves data-free knowledge distillation (DFKD) by generating a spectrum of data samples with similar distributions while retaining controlled variations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The innovative method generates a spectrum of data samples with similar distributions while retaining controlled variations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed diverse diffusion augmentation (DDA) approach improves data-free knowledge distillation (DFKD) by generating a spectrum of data samples with similar distributions while retaining controlled variations.
alignments.rationale: This contribution is repeated verbatim in the publication's description of DDA.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of an image filtering technique based on cosine similarity helps maintain fidelity during the knowledge distillation process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of an image filtering technique based on cosine similarity helps maintain fidelity during the knowledge distillation process.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this methodological contribution directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms contemporary state-of-the-art data-free knowledge distillation methods.

alignments.rationale: The publication adds detail about the variety of teacher-student configurations but preserves the core empirical superiority claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Conventional DFKD methods are limited by inadequate diversity and discrepancies in distribution between synthesized and original datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two-scale Graph Neural Operator (GNO), LatticeGraphNet (LGN), serves as a surrogate model for nonlinear finite-element simulations of three-dimensional latticed parts and structures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The two-scale graph neural operator, LatticeGraphNet (LGN), serves as a surrogate model for nonlinear finite-element simulations of three-dimensional latticed parts and structures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Text and contribution are effectively identical; no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LGN can predict deformation for arbitrary lattices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LGN can predict deformation for arbitrary lattices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and meaning are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach significantly reduces inference time while maintaining high accuracy for unseen simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach significantly reduces inference time while maintaining reasonable accuracy for unseen simulations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Core claim about speed and retained accuracy is unchanged, but publication tones accuracy down from 'high' to 'reasonable', indicating reduced confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study establishes the use of GNOs as efficient surrogate models for evaluating mechanical responses of lattices and structures. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study establishes the use of graph neural operators as efficient surrogate models for evaluating mechanical responses of lattices and structures. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim content is the same but the publication lowers the stated certainty (high -> medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoGCN demonstrates effectiveness in constructing optimal GCN architectures for Human Activity Recognition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoGCN demonstrates effectiveness in constructing optimal GCN architectures for Human Activity Recognition. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoGCN outperforms conventional NAS and GCN methods, as well as random search. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoGCN outperforms conventional NAS and GCN methods, as well as random search. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings highlight the significance of a diverse search space and an expressive input representation to enhance network performance and generalizability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings highlight the significance of a diverse search space and an expressive input representation for achieving good model performance and generalizability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conceptual conclusion with minor wording change emphasizing 'achieving good model performance'; no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning-based framework enables the fusion of multimodal data with heterogeneous dimensionality, such as 3D+2D. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep learning-based framework allows for the fusion of multimodal data with heterogeneous dimensionality, such as 3D + 2D. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework is compatible with localization tasks, addressing a limitation of current segmentation methods. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework is compatible with localization tasks, addressing a limitation of current segmentation methods. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Directly matched claim with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art monomodal methods on geographic atrophy and retinal blood vessel segmentation by up to 3.10% and 4.64% Dice, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art monomodal methods on geographic atrophy and retinal blood vessel segmentation by up to 3.10% and 4.64% Dice, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed phrase grounding-based style transfer (PGST) approach enhances a model's generalizability to multiple unseen target domains using only data from a single source domain during training. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed phrase-grounding-based style transfer (PGST) approach enhances a model's generalization to multiple unseen target domains using only data from a single source domain during training. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution statement is equivalent across preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The style-transferred source visual features are semantically rich and could be close to imaginary counterparts in the target domain. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The style-transferred visual features from the source domain are semantically rich and closely approximate those of their hypothetical counterparts in the target domain. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The detector could be effectively generalized to unseen target domains using only a single source domain for training by introducing imaginary counterparts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method significantly improves mean average precision (mAP), with an average increase of 8.8% across five diverse weather-driving benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication replaces the generic 'state-of-the-art' wording with a quantified mAP improvement across the same five benchmarks, clarifying and specifying the original performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PT-ME solves a new task at each iteration, effectively covering the continuous space. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PT-ME solves a new task at each iteration, effectively covering the continuous space. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: PT-ME exploits a new variation operator based on local linear regression. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: PT-ME outperforms all baselines, including the deep reinforcement learning algorithm PPO on two parametric-task toy problems and a robotic problem in simulation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: This study expands the definition of representational harms to include cognitive and affective states.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is effectively identical in content and emphasis; only minor wording differences ('impacts on' vs no extra words) exist.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Large language models are uniquely vulnerable to perpetrating representational harms, especially when these harms are unmeasured and unmitigated.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large language models are uniquely vulnerable to representational harms, especially when these harms are unmeasured and unmitigated.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The research aims to translate insights from fairness research into practical measurement and mitigation practices.

alignments.pub_claim_text: APT-Pipe is an automated prompt-tuning pipeline designed to enhance ChatGPT's text classification performance.

alignments.pub_claim_text: APT-Pipe can be extended to support additional tuning mechanisms.

alignments.pub_claim_text: GPT-4's responses achieved better scores than all the other LLM responses and human responses on relevance, correctness, helpfulness, and safety.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLM responses occasionally suffer from a lack of interpretation in one's medical context, incorrect statements, and lack of references.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLM responses occasionally suffered from lack of interpretation in one's medical context, incorrect statements, and lack of references.

alignments.pub_claim_text: GPT-4's responses achieved better scores than all the other LLM responses and human responses on relevance, correctness, helpfulness, and safety.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's unfairness in a downstream task significantly dropped after data repair using Chameleon.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model demonstrates superior performance over existing segmentation and reconstruction methods in terms of accuracy and continuity. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: FVIB can simultaneously maximize an approximation of the objective function for Variational Information Bottleneck (VIB) across all values of reasonable β. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Fine-grained Contrastive Enhancement (FCE) module enhances attention towards trajectory features by contrastive learning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Generative models leave unique traces in synthetic data that can be exploited to improve deepfake detectors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There exists a unique 'discriminative fingerprint' embedded in specific combinations of Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) coefficients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There exists a unique 'discriminative fingerprint' embedded in specific combinations of Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) coefficients.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The traces left by generative models are more discriminative and persistent under JPEG compression attacks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed co-orchestration approach enhances the measurement of complex observables by integrating dimensionality reduction and representation learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed co-orchestration approach integrates dimensionality reduction by variational autoencoders with representation learning for control over the latent space structure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The orchestrating agent in the co-orchestration approach selects measurement modalities based on anticipated knowledge gain and measurement cost.

alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the broader benefit (expediting exploration via modality interplay) rather than the preprint's specific agent-selection policy (knowledge gain/cost), shifting the framing and broadening the claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Multimodal co-orchestration expedites the exploration of combinatorial libraries by leveraging real-time insights from one modality to accelerate the discovery of others.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction result selection (PRS) algorithm significantly decreases wrong predictions when using the segment anything model (SAM). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Error-filtered SAM (EF-SAM) can exclude wrong masks remarkably with the error-filtering algorithm. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication renames and frames the PRS algorithm as an error-filtering component (EF-SAM) but retains the substantive claim that wrong masks are markedly reduced.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that FSS-SAM outperforms base few-shot semantic segmentation methods in both quantitative and qualitative aspects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiment results show that EF-SAM outperforms both the original FSS method and vanilla SAM method in both qualitative and quantitative aspects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same performance improvement but explicitly compares EF-SAM to both the original FSS and vanilla SAM, broadening the comparative scope while clarifying the method name.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) is proposed for classifying seventeen varieties of red and white grape variants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed convolutional neural network (CNN) achieved approximately 99% overall accuracy in classifying seventeen different varieties of grape cultivars.
alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same proposed CNN for classifying seventeen grape varieties; the publication restates the proposal and result without changing meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fitted model achieved approximately 99% overall accuracy, significantly outperforming previous works that barely achieved 81% overall accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluated metrics for the proposed method showed much better results compared to previous works, which barely achieved 81% overall accuracy over UAV imagery.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the high (~99%) performance and explicitly compares it to prior works (~81%), matching the preprint's combined result-and-comparison claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed CNN model is much more lightweight, with a reduced number of input bands and trainable weights, leading to reduced training time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CNN model is much more lightweight, using only 40 input bands and 560 k parameters, which significantly reduces training time to an average of 1 hour.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the lightweight / reduced-training-time claim but adds quantitative details (40 bands, 560k params, ~1 hour), which clarifies and strengthens confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates efficiency and robustness across different hyperspectral data sources.
alignments.rationale: This explicit claim about efficiency and robustness across different hyperspectral sources does not appear in the preprint and is newly stated in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Leveraging deep learning for end-to-end optimization in physical layer communication significantly enhances adaptability and performance in complex wireless environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Leveraging deep learning for end-to-end optimization in the physical layer significantly enhances adaptability and performance in complex wireless environments.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level result with essentially identical wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evolution of data-driven physical layer optimization enables advanced semantic applications across various modalities, elevating communication from bit-level to semantic-level intelligence.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evolution of data-driven physical layer optimization enables advanced semantic applications across various modalities, elevating communication from bit-level to semantic-level intelligence.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this forward-looking claim verbatim, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comprehensive studies that integrate both end-to-end communication and semantic communication systems remain significantly underexplored.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Comprehensive studies that integrate both end-to-end communication and semantic communication systems remain significantly underexplored.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the limitation/observation as stated in the preprint without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This article provides a comprehensive review of the emerging field of data-driven physical layer for end-to-end communication systems, emphasizing their role in enabling semantic applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This article provides a comprehensive review of the emerging field of data-driven physical layer for end-to-end communication systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the contribution claim (comprehensive review) but omits the explicit emphasis on enabling semantic applications, a modest clarification/streamlining of emphasis.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The review identifies key challenges and potential research directions for future advancements in deep learning for end-to-end communication and semantic communication systems.
alignments.rationale: Identification of specific challenges and future research directions is an explicit addition in the publication that is not present in the preprint's listed claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study develops a precise model that captures the sequential patterns and dependencies in travel data for accurate predictions of individual travelers' future destinations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model architecture based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) enables accurate predictions of individual travelers’ future destinations.
alignments.rationale: Both assert that the LSTM-based model captures sequential dependencies to enable accurate destination prediction; the publication reiterates the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel model architecture with a sliding window approach based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) is proposed for destination prediction in the transportation industry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model architecture based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) enables accurate predictions of individual travelers’ future destinations.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim of an LSTM-based architecture but omits the specific 'sliding window' novelty phrasing, clarifying the description while losing one architectural detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results highlight satisfactory performance and high scores achieved by the proposed model across different data sizes and performance metrics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate satisfactory performance and high scores achieved by the proposed model across different data sizes and performance metrics.
alignments.rationale: The experimental-result claim is restated in the publication with equivalent scope and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research contributes to advancing destination prediction methods, empowering companies to deliver personalized recommendations and optimize customer experiences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research contributes to advancing destination prediction methods in the transportation industry.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the contribution claim but omits the explicit business-impact phrasing (empowering companies to deliver personalized recommendations), narrowing the claimed downstream impact.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The LSTM model has a superior ability to capture complex temporal dependencies in travel data compared to other models.
alignments.rationale: The comparative superiority versus other models is stated in the publication but was not an explicit claim in the preprint, so it is an added assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study trained six models to identify the phosphorus removal process and created a simulator for the DRL environment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study trained six models to identify the phosphorus removal process and created a simulator for the DRL environment.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution claim verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models achieved high accuracy (>97%) in identifying the phosphorus removal process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models achieved high accuracy (> 97%) in one-step ahead prediction of the test dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that the >97% accuracy pertains to one-step-ahead test predictions, narrowing the temporal scope compared to the broader preprint phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Uncertainty and incorrect prediction behavior limited the models' performance as simulators over longer horizons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models struggled as simulators over longer horizons, showing uncertainty and incorrect predictions in multi-step simulations.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication describe the same limitation: uncertainty and incorrect behavior in multi-step/long-horizon simulations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Compounding errors in the models' predictions were identified as one of the causes of performance limitations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Compounding errors in the models’ predictions were identified as one of the causes of the simulation problems.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same causal attribution (compounding errors) for the observed simulation issues.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper proposes a novel data-driven approach for unsupervised semantic segmentation (DatUS^2) as a downstream task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DatUS generates semantically consistent pseudosegmentation masks for an unlabeled image dataset without using visual prior or synchronized data.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same proposed data-driven unsupervised segmentation framework (DatUS/DatUS^2) and its role; the core contribution is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DatUS^2 generates semantically consistent and dense pseudo annotate segmentation masks for the unlabeled image dataset without using any visual-prior or synchronized data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DatUS generates semantically consistent pseudosegmentation masks for an unlabeled image dataset without using visual prior or synchronized data.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution claim about generating semantically consistent pseudomasks without priors or synchronization with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best version of DatUS^2 outperforms the existing state-of-the-art method for the unsupervised dense semantic segmentation task with 15.02% MiOU and 21.47% Pixel accuracy on the SUIM dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DatUS outperforms state-of-the-art methods for the unsupervised dense semantic segmentation task with improvements of 15.02% MIoU, 21.47% pixel accuracy, and 16.06% average F1 score on the validation set of the SUIM dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the performance improvements but clarifies that the reported gains are on the validation set and adds an additional F1 improvement; specifying validation set narrows the original phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DatUS^2 achieves a competitive level of accuracy for a large-scale and complex dataset, i.e., the COCO dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework achieves a competitive level of accuracy for a large-scale COCO dataset.
alignments.rationale: Both versions assert comparable competitive performance on COCO; the publication restates this result with equivalent scope.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework, DatUS, achieves the highest MIoU of 24.90 and average F1 score of 36.3 by choosing DINOv2 on the training set of the SUIM dataset.
alignments.rationale: The specific numeric best-results claim (exact MIoU/F1 values and the use of DINOv2) appears in the publication but was not stated in the preprint's listed claims, so it is an added assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces a straightforward black-box method for efficiently crafting jailbreak prompts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study introduces a straightforward black-box method for efficiently crafting jailbreak prompts that bypass LLM defenses.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific target (bypassing LLM defenses) to the same contribution, making the claim more specific but not changing its core meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method consistently achieved an attack success rate exceeding 80% within an average of five iterations for forbidden questions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method consistently achieved an attack success rate exceeding 80% within an average of five iterations for forbidden questions.
alignments.rationale: The result is verbatim between preprint and publication, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The jailbreak prompts generated were naturally-worded, succinct, and challenging to defend against.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The jailbreak prompts generated were naturally worded, succinct, and challenging to defend against.
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively identical (minor punctuation/spacing); the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings suggest that the creation of effective jailbreak prompts is less complex than previously believed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings suggest that the creation of effective jailbreak prompts is less complex than previously believed.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried over without change in content or strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study underscores the heightened risk posed by black-box jailbreak attacks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study underscores the heightened risk posed by black-box jailbreak attacks.
alignments.rationale: The concluding risk statement is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized the field of natural language processing with their ability to understand and generate human-like text.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Large language models (LLMs) have revolutionized the field of natural language processing with their ability to understand and generate human-like text.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a pressing need to address the computational challenges associated with the scale and complexity of LLMs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a pressing need to address the computational challenges associated with the scale and complexity of LLMs.
alignments.rationale: Text is the same; only the publication labels it as a 'limitation' in metadata but the claim text and strength remain unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper presents a comprehensive survey on hardware accelerators designed to enhance the performance and energy efficiency of Large Language Models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper presents a comprehensive survey of hardware accelerators designed to enhance the performance and energy efficiency of large language models.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is the same aside from minor wording/capitalization differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey provides valuable insights for researchers, engineers, and decision-makers aiming to optimize the deployment of LLMs in real-world applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The survey provides valuable insights for researchers, engineers, and decision-makers aiming to optimize the deployment of LLMs in real-world applications.
alignments.rationale: The concluding utility claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed fast graph-based denoising (FGBD) method significantly reduces processing time for large-scale point clouds while maintaining accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed fast graph-based denoising (FGBD) method significantly reduces processing time for large-scale point clouds while maintaining accuracy.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Denoising was successfully performed at 30 frames per second with frames containing approximately 1 million points.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new low-cost filter selection method enhances denoising accuracy to compensate for degradation caused by acceleration algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new low-cost filter selection method enhances denoising accuracy to compensate for degradation caused by acceleration algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Locally Adaptive Adversarial Color Attack (LAACA) significantly degrades the generation quality of neural style transfer (NST) while maintaining an acceptable level of visual change in the original image.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Locally Adaptive Adversarial Color Attack (LAACA) significantly degrades the generation quality of neural style transfer (NST) while maintaining an acceptable level of visual change in the original image.
alignments.rationale: The LAACA result is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Adversarial Color Distance Metric (ACDM) can efficiently measure color-mattered tasks, which is crucial in the context of artistic works.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Aesthetic Color Distance Metric (ACDM) can efficiently measure color-mattered tasks, which is crucial in the context of artistic works.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LAACA empowers artists to protect their artwork from unauthorized style transfer by processing before public release.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LAACA empowers artists to protect their artwork from unauthorized style transfer by processing before public release.
alignments.rationale: The stated protective use-case is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing metrics often overlook the importance of color fidelity in evaluating color-mattered tasks, such as the quality of NST-generated images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing metrics often overlook the importance of color fidelity in evaluating color-mattered tasks, such as the quality of NST-generated images.
alignments.rationale: The limitation claim is carried over without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey reviews over 500 publications related to learning from graphs with heterophily.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The survey reviews over 500 publications related to learning from graphs with heterophily.
alignments.rationale: The high-level survey scope and magnitude claim are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: More than 340 of the reviewed publications are directly related to heterophilic graphs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: More than 300 of the reviewed publications are directly related to heterophilic graphs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey categorizes existing methods based on a hierarchical taxonomy including GNN models, learning paradigms, and practical applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing methods for learning from heterophilic graphs are systematically categorized based on a hierarchical taxonomy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey discusses the primary challenges of existing studies on graph heterophily.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The survey discusses the primary challenges of existing studies on graph heterophily.
alignments.rationale: The discussion of challenges remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey highlights promising avenues for future research in the field of graph heterophily.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Promising avenues for future research in graph heterophily are highlighted.
alignments.rationale: The future-work highlight is equivalent in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adversarial Pruning (Adv-Pruning) enhances the dodging capabilities of adversarial examples while preserving their impersonation capabilities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adversarial Pruning (Adv-Pruning) enhances the dodging capabilities of adversarial examples while preserving their impersonation capabilities. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim with the same certainty and role, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method includes stages of Priming, Pruning, and Restoration to fine-tune existing adversarial examples. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method includes stages of Priming, Pruning, and Restoration to fine-tune existing adversarial examples. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same methodological description and certainty as the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comprehensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of Adv-Pruning compared to state-of-the-art adversarial attack methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Comprehensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of Adv-Pruning compared to state-of-the-art adversarial attack methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental superiority claim is carried over identically to the publication with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modified DeepONet significantly improves accuracy, efficiency, and robustness in solving Riemann problems for compressible flows. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modified DeepONet significantly improves accuracy, efficiency, and robustness in solving Riemann problems for compressible flows. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this key performance claim exactly and with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hierarchical data-driven basis produced by the modified DeepONet reflects all flow features necessary for accurate solutions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hierarchical data-driven basis produced by the modified DeepONet reflects all flow features necessary for accurate solutions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The U-Net based neural operator provides very accurate solutions for Riemann problems at large pressure ratios, but is computationally more expensive. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The U-Net based neural operator provides very accurate solutions for Riemann problems at high-pressure ratios, but is computationally more expensive. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording changed slightly from 'large' to 'high' pressure ratios but the technical content and certainty remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simple neural network architectures, when properly pre-trained, can achieve very accurate solutions of Riemann problems for real-time forecasting. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Simple neural network architectures, when properly pre-trained, can achieve very accurate solutions of Riemann problems for real-time forecasting. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this conclusion identically, including its certainty and claimed application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate subwavelength imaging of phase and amplitude objects based on all-optical diffractive encoding and decoding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate subwavelength imaging of phase and amplitude objects based on all-optical diffractive encoding and decoding. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core demonstration claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diffractive imager uses a thin, high-index solid-immersion layer to transmit high-frequency information of the object. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The diffractive imager uses a thin, high-index solid-immersion layer to transmit high-frequency information of the object. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing the solid-immersion layer is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This all-optical collaboration between a diffractive solid-immersion encoder and the following decoder layers in air can resolve subwavelength phase and amplitude features of input objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This all-optical collaboration can resolve subwavelength phase and amplitude features of input objects in a highly compact design. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the qualification 'in a highly compact design,' clarifying the form factor and thereby narrowing the claim to that compact implementation while preserving the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrated phase-to-intensity transformations and all-optically reconstructed subwavelength phase features of input objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrated phase-to-intensity transformations and all-optically reconstructed subwavelength phase features of input objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental demonstrations are restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This solid-immersion-based diffractive imager can find wide-ranging applications in bioimaging, endoscopy, sensing and materials characterization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This solid-immersion-based diffractive imager can find wide-ranging applications in bioimaging, endoscopy, sensing and materials characterization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The suggested application domains and certainty are preserved in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Supervised Magnitude-Altitude Scoring (SMAS) methodology effectively combines gene selection based on statistical significance and expression changes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SMAS methodology is effective in revealing complex genetic interactions and response mechanisms essential for advancing the development of diagnostic tools and therapeutic strategies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the effectiveness and implications of SMAS but omits the specific phrasing about combining selection criteria; the methodological detail is subsumed into a broader claim about effectiveness and impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IFI6 and IFI27 are identified as critical biomarkers with 100% accuracy in classifying various stages of Ebola infection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of SMAS led to the identification of IFI6 and IFI27 as key biomarkers, which demonstrated perfect predictive performance with 100% accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The identification and reported 100% predictive performance for IFI6 and IFI27 are explicitly carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Genes including MX1, OAS1, and ISG15 were significantly upregulated, highlighting their essential roles in the immune response to EBOV. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Genes including MX1, OAS1, and ISG15 were significantly upregulated, underscoring their vital roles in the immune response to EBOV. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The upregulation claim is the same, but the publication presents the result with higher certainty, increasing the emphasis on their roles.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SMAS method reveals complex genetic interactions and response mechanisms during EBOV infection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SMAS methodology is effective in revealing complex genetic interactions and response mechanisms essential for advancing the development of diagnostic tools and therapeutic strategies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that SMAS reveals complex interactions but expands the stated downstream relevance to development of diagnostics and therapeutics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research provides valuable insights into EBOV pathogenesis and aids in developing more precise diagnostic tools and therapeutic strategies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study provides valuable insights into EBOV pathogenesis, demonstrating the potential of SMAS to enhance the precision of diagnostics and interventions for Ebola and other viral infections. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the claim (higher certainty) and broadens its scope to include potential applicability to other viral infections, increasing its stated impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep autoencoder method for anomaly detection is effective in identifying interesting examples, such as interstellar objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep autoencoder method demonstrates efficacy in detecting anomalies in solar system object data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using the autoencoder allows for the discovery of further examples of interesting classes of objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The autoencoder approach successfully identifies interesting examples, including interstellar objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim that the autoencoder identifies interesting objects, explicitly mentioning interstellar objects as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Classic unsupervised approaches to anomaly detection have limitations, as demonstrated through the generation of synthetic anomalies. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Classic unsupervised approaches to anomaly detection have limitations, as shown through the generation of synthetic anomalies. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation of classic unsupervised approaches is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The feasibility of using a supervised learning approach for anomaly detection is evaluated. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future work should focus on expanding the feature space to uncover a greater variety of anomalies using the autoencoder. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future work should expand the feature space to uncover a greater variety of anomalies using the autoencoder. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present a new vision-haptic dataset (DaFoEs) with variable soft environments for the training of deep neural models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present a new vision-haptic dataset (DaFoEs) with variable soft environments for the training of deep neural models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Single dataset training tends to overfit to the training data domain and has difficulties translating results across new domains. (certainty=high, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Dataset mixing presents a good translation with a mean relative estimated force error of 5 and 12 for the recurrent and non-recurrent models respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method marginally increases the effectiveness of transformers for force estimation up to a maximum of approximately 15% as the volume of available data increases by 150. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Mixing experimental setups for vision-state force estimation in MIRS is a possible approach towards the general solution of the problem. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed graph neural network architecture substitutes classical message passing with an analysis of the local distribution of node features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed graph neural network architecture substitutes classical message passing with an analysis of the local distribution of node features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model effectively creates a message passing-like mechanism where the message is determined by the ensemble of the features. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model effectively creates a message passing-like mechanism where the message is determined by the ensemble of the features. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the generalizability claim to large- and very large-scale practical routing problems, more precisely defining the intended application scale.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The initial experimental conditions significantly influence the learning dynamics of Deep Kernel Learning (DKL) in Autonomous Experimentation (AE).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The initial experimental conditions significantly influence the learning dynamics of Deep Kernel Learning in Autonomous Experimentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about the influence of initial conditions on DKL learning dynamics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The concept of 'seed effect' demonstrates that the initial experiment setup impacts the subsequent learning trajectory.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The concept of the 'seed effect' demonstrates that the initial experiment setup impacts the subsequent learning trajectory.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim about the seed effect with equivalent emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In-loop seed interventions allow operators to influence the exploration process in AE.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In-loop seed interventions allow operators to effectively influence the exploration process in Autonomous Experimentation.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the qualifier 'effectively,' slightly strengthening the operational claim while preserving its intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study illustrates the effectiveness of DKL in predicting material properties when considering the 'seed effect' and in-loop seed interventions.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not assert DKL's predictive effectiveness for material properties; it reframes results around learning rates and efficiency rather than prediction performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Initial choices and adaptive interventions are crucial for optimizing learning rates and enhancing the efficiency of automated material characterization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study illustrates that initial choices and adaptive interventions optimize learning rates and enhance the efficiency of automated material characterization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Scheduled Curiosity-Deep Dyna-Q (SC-DDQ) framework leads to a significant improvement over the Deep Dyna-Q (DDQ) and Deep Q-learning (DQN) models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Scheduled Curiosity-Deep Dyna-Q (SC-DDQ) framework leads to a significant improvement over the Deep Dyna-Q (DDQ) and Deep Q-learning (DQN) models.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional curriculum learning was not always effective in the context of dialog agent training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional curriculum learning was not always effective in the context of dialog agent training.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about traditional curriculum learning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The easy-first and difficult-first strategies are more suitable for the SC-DDQ and DDQ frameworks, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The easy-first and difficult-first strategies are more suitable for the SC-DDQ and DDQ frameworks, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training strategies with high entropy in the first stage and low entropy in the last stage lead to better performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Training strategies with high entropy in the first stage and low entropy in the last stage lead to better performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same experimental finding with equal certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces an advanced approach for recognizing ingredients segmented from food images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study introduces a new approach for recognizing multiple ingredients segmented from food images.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that the approach targets multiple ingredients, clarifying the original contribution while preserving its intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel model pruning method is proposed that enhances the efficiency of the classification model.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention model pruning or a pruning-based efficiency improvement, so this specific contribution is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results validate the effectiveness of our method, particularly highlighting its improved capability in recognizing multiple ingredients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results validate the effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed method in recognizing multiple ingredients.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds efficiency to the experimental validation, slightly broadening the measured outcomes while matching the core experimental claim about multiple-ingredient recognition.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The single-ingredient image dataset encompasses 9982 images across 110 diverse categories, emphasizing variety in ingredient shapes.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim reports the dataset size or composition; dataset details from the preprint are not represented among the published summary claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Pointwise Distance Distribution (PDD) effectively distinguishes all periodic crystals in the Cambridge Structural Database as purely periodic sets of points without atomic types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PDD distinguished all more than 660 thousand periodic crystals in the Cambridge Structural Database as purely periodic sets of points without atomic types.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed transformer model with a modified self-attention mechanism combines PDD with compositional information via a spatial encoding method.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the architectural detail about the modified self-attention or the specific combination of PDD and composition, so this preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The transformer model produces accuracy on par with state-of-the-art methods while being several times faster in both training and prediction time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed transformer model produces accuracy on par with state-of-the-art methods while being several times faster in both training and prediction time.
alignments.rationale: The wording and meaning are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Pointwise Distance Distribution (PDD) is an effective representation for periodic crystals in machine learning algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a more general statement framing PDD as an effective ML representation; this general framing is not separately stated in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed image transformation technique renders facial images unrecognizable to the eye while maintaining their identifiability by neural network models.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same core contribution claim without substantive change.

alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention storage-for-verification specifically, so this application-oriented claim appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The distortion can change the image content by more than 70% while maintaining the same recognition accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The numerical result and conclusion are preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method can be applied in various artificial intelligence applications to distort visual data while keeping derived features close.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology can be applied in various artificial intelligence applications to distort visual data while keeping derived features close.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same general applicability claim with similar certainty.

alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a claim about source-code release, so this contribution appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method was compared with previously state-of-the-art approaches.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Fine-tuned Llama models demonstrated high performance across various metrics.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The newly developed SimpleDC corpus will serve as a valuable asset to the research community, particularly in patient education simplification.

alignments.pub_claim_text: TA-RNN showed superior performance on the Medical Information Mart for Intensive Care (MIMIC-III) dataset for mortality prediction.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated method for quantifying RNAscope transcripts performed similarly to expert annotators, achieving an F1-score of 0.571 compared to the expert inter-rater F1-score of 0.596. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives numerical F1 scores that clarify the preprint's statement of similar performance, without changing the substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feature analysis showed that a small set of grey level features were well suited for automatically segmenting and classifying the positions of RNAscope transcripts from breast cancer tissue. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A small set of gray level features, including Gray Level Dependence Matrix and Neighbouring Gray Tone Difference Matrix features, are well suited for the task of quantifying RNAscope transcripts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication names specific feature families and thus clarifies and slightly specifies the preprint's feature-analysis claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results demonstrate the potential of grey level texture features for automated quantification of RNAscope in the pathology workflow. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate the potential of gray level texture features for automated quantification of RNAscope in the pathology workflow. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same concluding claim about the potential of gray-level texture features with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep generative models complement Markov-chain-Monte-Carlo methods for efficiently sampling from high-dimensional distributions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results demonstrate improved performance of the conditional normalising flows compared to traditional methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's framing that generative models 'complement MCMC' is changed in the publication to a demonstrable performance improvement versus traditional methods, shifting the claim from complementarity to empirical improvement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We systematically study central problems in conditional Normalising Flows, such as high variance, mode collapse, and data efficiency. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method reduces mode collapse in conditional normalising flows. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint described a systematic study of problems; the publication emphasizes a specific result (reduction of mode collapse), shifting from a study claim to a concrete outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose adversarial training for Normalising Flows to ameliorate problems like high variance and mode collapse. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adversarial learning is effectively integrated into the framework of conditional normalising flows. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: What was proposed in the preprint is presented in the publication as effectively integrated into the framework, which clarifies the contribution and increases emphasis on effective integration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments are conducted with low-dimensional synthetic datasets and XY spin models in two spatial dimensions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not explicitly mention the described experimental domains; the preprint's specific experimental-description claim is not present among the publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AccessLens is an end-to-end system designed to identify inaccessible interfaces in daily objects. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AccessLens is an end-to-end system designed to identify inaccessible interfaces in daily objects. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint contribution identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AccessDB dataset is designed to automatically recognize 21 distinct Inaccessibility Classes within 6 common object categories. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AccessDB dataset is designed to automatically recognize 21 distinct Inaccessibility Classes within 6 common object categories. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The dataset claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate our detector's performance in detecting inaccessible objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate our detector’s performance in detecting inaccessible objects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental performance claim is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Leveraging low-cost 3D-printed augmentations such as knob magnifiers and tactile labels seems promising for enhancing accessibility. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Leveraging low-cost 3D-printed augmentations such as knob magnifiers and tactile labels seems promising for enhancing accessibility. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same concluding suggestion about 3D-printed augmentations with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Accurate and automated gland segmentation on pathological images can assist pathologists in diagnosing the malignancy of colorectal adenocarcinoma. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Accurate and automated gland segmentation on pathological images can assist pathologists in diagnosing the malignancy of colorectal adenocarcinoma. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion verbatim with the same certainty and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed DEA model consists of two branches: the backbone encoding and decoding network and the local semantic extraction network. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed DEA model consists of two branches: the backbone encoding and decoding network and the local semantic extraction network. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution description is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results on two public datasets, GlaS and CRAG, confirm that the performance of our method is better than other gland segmentation methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results on two public datasets, GlaS and CRAG, confirm that the performance of our method is better than other gland segmentation methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported experimental result and comparison remain the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A real-time emotion detection EEG-based system was developed to interpret and classify specific emotions aligned with Descartes' early proposal. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A real-time emotion recognition EEG-based system was developed to interpret and classify specific emotions aligned with Descartes' early proposal. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication uses slightly different wording ('recognition' vs 'detection') but conveys the same developed system; this is a minor clarification in wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed model achieved a general accuracy of 96%, which is higher than the previously reported accuracy of 88% in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed machine learning model achieved a general accuracy of 94%, which is higher than the previously reported accuracy of 88%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core comparative result remains but the reported accuracy drops from 96% to 94%, so the claim is revised and its confidence is slightly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model can classify more than 15 emotions based on the VAD values obtained, extending the range of its application. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model can classify more than 15 emotions based on the VAD values obtained. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core capability claim (>15 emotions) but removes the extra phrasing about extending application range, a minor clarification/narrowing of the original phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This project addresses the gap in educational tools for the humanities by integrating technology and emotional monitoring in immersive learning environments. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of emotional data into the Neurohumanities Lab interactive platform aims to create a comprehensive and immersive learning environment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's stronger 'addresses the gap' contribution as an aim of platform integration, softening the claim (tempering confidence) while preserving the same direction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Remote novices cannot fully utilize online resources for troubleshooting 3D printing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Remote novices cannot fully utilize online resources for troubleshooting 3D printing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 3DPFIX helped participants spend significantly less effort in diagnosing failures and finding more accurate solutions compared to their common practice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 3DPFIX helped participants spend significantly less effort in diagnosing failures and finding more accurate solutions compared to their common practice. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental finding is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 3DPFIX users learn about 3D printing domain-specific knowledge. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 3DPFIX users learn about 3D printing domain-specific knowledge. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the learning outcome claim as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The online archives provide general information that makes it hard for novices to relate their unique cases with existing descriptions. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The online archives provide general information that makes it hard for novices to relate their unique cases with existing descriptions. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is expressed identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hyperparameter settings have a considerable impact on the performance of the CMA-ES, especially for difficult tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hyperparameter settings of CMA-ES have a considerable impact on performance, especially for difficult tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result about hyperparameter sensitivity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A small learning rate is necessary for the performance of the CMA-ES. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A small learning rate is necessary for optimal CMA-ES performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording differs slightly ('performance' vs 'optimal performance') but the conclusion is effectively the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed learning rate adaptation mechanism for the CMA-ES maintains a constant signal-to-noise ratio. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel learning rate adaptation mechanism for CMA-ES maintains a constant signal-to-noise ratio. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is the same; the publication labels it explicitly as novel but the content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CMA-ES with the proposed learning rate adaptation works well for multimodal and/or noisy problems without extremely expensive learning rate tuning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CMA-ES with the proposed learning rate adaptation works well for multimodal and/or noisy problems without extremely expensive learning rate tuning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported result and its conditions remain the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed AC-DRL algorithm generates 23%, 20%, and 11% higher revenue compared to two optimization-based and one DRL-based benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed AC-DRL algorithm outperforms two optimization-based and one DRL-based benchmarks by generating 23, 20, and 11 higher revenue, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance improvements are the same between preprint and publication, despite minor punctuation differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The excess solar generation can effectively charge the BESS to bid in the market. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The excess solar generation can effectively charge the BESS to bid in the market, significantly reducing solar curtailments by 76. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication combines this conclusion with an explicit quantified curtailment reduction, thereby broadening the published claim to include an additional measured outcome.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The strategy significantly reduces solar curtailments by 76%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The excess solar generation can effectively charge the BESS to bid in the market, significantly reducing solar curtailments by 76. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The exact reduction figure and result are preserved; the publication merges this result with the charging/market-bidding statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The synergy of the solar-battery energy storage system enhances its economic viability. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The synergy of the solar-battery energy storage system enhances its economic potential by serving as a backup to reduce solar curtailments while participating in the electricity market. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the mechanisms (serving as backup and market participation) through which the synergy enhances economic potential, broadening the explanatory scope of the original conclusion.
